what: Resubmit v188 baseline unchanged to establish fresh LB score — previous resubmit was v188 at unknown date; server/cache state may have changed since competition midpoint
why: 225+ attempts exhausted all viable optimization paths. Need a fresh baseline LB score to determine if server-side changes (Triton cache, ROCm updates, thermal conditions) have shifted the performance landscape. If score improves, it confirms external improvement; if same, confirms we are at the optimization ceiling for current approach.

what: BSM=32 BSN=128 BSK=256 NW=4 NS=2 waves=1 .cg for M=64 fused path — larger M tile (32 rows) covers M=64 in 2 M-blocks; full MFMA 16x16 utilization per tile. Grid=112 blocks (0.37 waves) vs current BSM=16 grid=224 (0.74 waves), but 2x larger tile amortizes quant overhead better
why: BSM=32 BSN=128 NW=4 never tested for M=64 fused. Prior BSM=32 tests used BSN=64 NW=2 (v81, +133%) or matrix_instr_nonkdim=32 (v131, +46%). BSM=32 BSN=128 NW=4 NS=2 matches the tile shape that works well for M<=32 K>1024 path, and the inline quant (_mxfp4_quant_op) may benefit from processing 32 rows at once. M=64 at 13.6us is the largest geomean contributor.

what_failed: Session 13: 2 approaches tested after extensive research (225+ prior attempts reviewed). (1) Baseline resubmit to check server changes — neutral (+0.9% noise, 9.09 vs 9.01). (2) BSM=32 BSN=128 for M=64 fused path — massive regression (+50%, 19.9 vs 13.3 BM) due to halving grid from 224 to 112 blocks, losing CU utilization. All known optimizations for this kernel are exhausted: tile configs, split-K, cache modifiers, ASM vs Triton, quant kernel tuning, reduce kernel variants, env vars, dispatch overhead, gluon kernels. The 9.5% gap to #1 (8.23us) likely requires a fundamentally different kernel implementation.
